title: Multi-Modal Fusion Transformer for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
authors: Aditya Prakash, Kashyap Chitta, and Andreas Geiger
summary: A representative end-to-end driving baseline that fuses camera and LiDAR for direct waypoint prediction. Useful as a canonical E2E reference in the fast-policy line.
fit_note: Pure E2E driving paper; fits category 1 only.

title: DriveAdapter: Breaking the Coupling Barrier of Perception and Planning in End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: A strong E2E driving paper that decouples perception and planning with teacher-student adaptation while keeping the overall AD pipeline end-to-end at inference.
fit_note: E2E driving paper, but not VLM/VLA and not a dual-branch E2E+VLM method.

title: Planning-Oriented Autonomous Driving
summary: A foundational planning-oriented E2E framework that unifies perception, prediction, and planning under a single driving objective.
fit_note: Pure E2E driving paper; fits category 1 only.

title: Think Twice before Driving: Towards Scalable Decoders for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: An E2E driving method that improves decoder design and planning quality, often cited as a strong planning-oriented E2E baseline.
fit_note: Pure E2E driving paper; fits category 1 only.

title: VAD: Vectorized Scene Representation for Efficient Autonomous Driving
summary: An efficient end-to-end driving framework built on vectorized scene representations, often cited for efficiency-oriented E2E planning.
fit_note: Pure E2E driving paper; fits category 1 only.

title: DriveGPT4: Interpretable End-to-end Autonomous Driving via Large Language Model
summary: An interpretable E2E autonomous driving framework that uses an LLM to connect multi-frame visual input, reasoning, QA, and low-level control.
fit_note: LLM-based E2E driving paper; not a separate dual-branch E2E+VLM design.

title: DriveLM: Driving with Graph Visual Question Answering
summary: A representative VLM-for-driving paper that formulates driving as graph VQA spanning perception, prediction, planning, behavior, and motion.
fit_note: Fits category 1 as VLM-for-driving work; not a dual-branch E2E+VLM paper.

title: Embodied Understanding of Driving Scenarios
summary: Introduces ELM, a VLM-style embodied understanding framework for driving scenes with spatial and temporal reasoning.
fit_note: Fits category 1 as VLM/embodied understanding for AD; not a dual-branch E2E+VLM paper.

title: LMDrive: Closed-Loop End-to-End Driving with Large Language Models
summary: A language-guided closed-loop E2E driving framework that adapts a frozen LLM with sensor encoders and adapters for direct control generation.
fit_note: Language-guided E2E driving, but not a separated dual-branch E2E+VLM design.

title: Reason2Drive: Towards Interpretable and Chain-based Reasoning for Autonomous Driving
summary: A benchmark and method paper for chain-based VLM reasoning in driving, emphasizing interpretable perception-prediction-reasoning chains.
fit_note: Fits category 1 as a major VLM reasoning paper for AD; not a dual-branch E2E+VLM paper.

title: Navigation-Guided Sparse Scene Representation for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: A recent E2E driving method that uses a sparse navigation-guided scene representation to reduce annotation burden and computation cost.
fit_note: Pure E2E driving paper; fits category 1 only.

title: ORION: A Holistic End-to-End Autonomous Driving Framework by Vision-Language Instructed Action Generation
summary: A unified E2E driving framework that combines long-term history aggregation, an LLM for scenario reasoning, and a generative planner.
fit_note: Fits category 1 as an E2E+LLM/VLM paper, but not category 2 because it is presented as a unified framework rather than two separate branches.

title: SimLingo: Vision-Only Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving with Language-Action Alignment
summary: A VLM-based closed-loop driving model that jointly targets driving, vision-language understanding, and language-action alignment.
fit_note: Fits category 1 as a strong VLA/VLM-style AD paper; not category 2 because it is not framed as a separate E2E-branch plus VLM-branch system.

title: DriveVLM: The Convergence of Autonomous Driving and Large Vision-Language Models
summary: A CoRL paper on VLM-based autonomous driving that also introduces DriveVLM-Dual, a hybrid deployment combining VLM reasoning with a conventional driving pipeline.
fit_note: Includes DriveVLM-Dual, a hybrid system that combines a VLM stack with a traditional driving pipeline; best fit for category 2.

title: Generalizing End-To-End Autonomous Driving In Real-World Environments Using Zero-Shot LLMs
summary: An efficient closed-loop architecture where a multimodal LLM periodically produces high-level instructions to guide an end-to-end driving model in the real world.
fit_note: Fits category 2 because the paper explicitly combines a multimodal LLM branch with an end-to-end driving model, and the LLM is queried at a slower rate than the E2E controller.

title: Senna: Bridging Large Vision-Language Models and End-to-End Autonomous Driving
authors: Bo Jiang, Shaoyu Chen, Bencheng Liao, Xingyu Zhang, Wei Yin, Qian Zhang, Chang Huang, Wenyu Liu, and Xinggang Wang
summary: A hybrid driving system that explicitly combines an LVLM (Senna-VLM) with an end-to-end model (Senna-E2E), decoupling high-level language planning from low-level trajectory prediction.
fit_note: Directly matches category 2: one LVLM branch for high-level planning and one E2E branch for trajectory prediction. Not used in category 3 because it does not center on an explicit switching trigger.

title: Distilling Multi-modal Large Language Models for Autonomous Driving
authors: Deepti Hegde, Rajeev Yasarla, Hong Cai, Shizhong Han, Apratim Bhattacharyya, Shweta Mahajan, Litian Liu, Risheek Garrepalli, Vishal M. Patel, and Fatih Porikli
summary: A CVPR 2025 framework that jointly trains a multi-modal LLM branch and a vision-based end-to-end planner, distilling the former into the latter to preserve efficiency.
fit_note: Fits category 2 because the method explicitly contains both an MLLM planning branch and a vision-based E2E planner under joint training. It is not category 3 because the paper does not frame its contribution as explicit online switching.

title: SOLVE: Synergy of Language-Vision and End-to-End Networks for Autonomous Driving
summary: A clear VLM+E2E hybrid that promotes feature-level and trajectory-level cooperation between a VLM branch and an end-to-end model.
fit_note: Strong category 2 match under your definition: it explicitly contains a VLM branch and an E2E branch.

title: ReCogDrive: A Reinforced Cognitive Framework for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
authors: Yongkang Li, Kaixin Xiong, Xiangyu Guo, Fang Li, Sixu Yan, Gangwei Xu, Lijun Zhou, Long Chen, Haiyang Sun, Bing Wang, Kun Ma, Guang Chen, Hangjun Ye, Wenyu Liu, and Xinggang Wang
summary: An ICLR 2026 framework that integrates a cognitive VLM / autoregressive reasoning model with a diffusion-based end-to-end planner, then further refines the planner with reinforcement learning.
fit_note: Fits category 2 because it explicitly combines a VLM reasoning component with a separate diffusion planner for end-to-end trajectory generation. It is not category 3 because expert selection is not its main switching mechanism.

title: Continuously Learning, Adapting, and Improving: A Dual-Process Approach to Autonomous Driving
summary: A dual-process autonomous driving system with a fast heuristic process and a slower analytic process, coupled with memory-based continual improvement.
fit_note: Included only in category 3. It is not a clean E2E+VLM dual-branch paper under your definition, but it does implement an explicit fallback from the fast process to the analytic process.

title: AdaThinkDrive: Adaptive Thinking via Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
summary: A recent autonomous driving VLA paper with Think / Non-Think modes and reinforcement learning to decide when deeper reasoning is worthwhile.
fit_note: Strong category 3 fit, but currently arXiv only.

title: AutoVLA: A Vision-Language-Action Model for End-to-End Autonomous Driving with Adaptive Reasoning and Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
summary: A VLA driving model with fast and slow thinking modes and adaptive reasoning learned through supervised and reinforcement fine-tuning.
fit_note: Fits category 3 because it explicitly supports adaptive switching between fast and slow reasoning modes.

title: Deliberation Meets Reaction: A Dual-Expert VLA framework for Autonomous Driving
summary: A recent dual-expert VLA for driving with a scene-aware router that dynamically chooses between a reactive expert and a deliberative expert.
fit_note: Very strong category 3 fit because it has an explicit scene-aware routing mechanism, but it is still a submission rather than an archival publication.
